explanation: The command uses explicit quotes around the pipe path, which is a safe mutation technique (insertion). The behavior remains unchanged, but the syntax is slightly modified to test for potential bypasses.
This is the original command with no mutation applied. It is included for comparison purposes.
The '>' redirection operator is escaped using the caret (^) character, which is a valid mutation technique (recoding/encoding). This tests whether escaping characters can bypass detection.
The command is simplified by removing unnecessary elements (omission), but it retains the core behavior of writing to a named pipe. This tests minimalistic bypass scenarios.
The entire command is wrapped in quotes (insertion), which may alter parsing behavior in some contexts. This tests whether quote insertion can bypass detection.
